report_name: Critical Unpatched RCE Flaw in Hugging Face's LeRobot AI Platform Puts Robotics Systems at Risk

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: An unauthenticated, network-adjacent attacker can exploit an insecure data deserialization vulnerability (CVE-2026-25874) in the public-facing LeRobot gRPC server to achieve remote code execution. The exploit involves sending a malicious pickle payload to specific gRPC calls like `SendPolicyInstructions`.

tools.name: LeRobot
tools.description: A popular open-source robotics platform from Hugging Face, which contains a critical unpatched RCE vulnerability (CVE-2026-25874) in versions 0.4.3 and prior.

tools.name: pickle
tools.description: The Python `pickle` module was used insecurely for data deserialization (`pickle.loads()`) over unauthenticated channels, which is the root cause of the RCE vulnerability.

tools.name: gRPC
tools.description: The LeRobot platform uses unauthenticated and unencrypted gRPC channels for communication between its policy server and robot client, allowing an attacker to send a malicious payload.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-25874
vulnerabilities.description: A critical untrusted data deserialization vulnerability in Hugging Face's LeRobot platform. The use of `pickle.loads()` on data from unauthenticated gRPC channels allows a network-adjacent attacker to achieve remote code execution.

identities.name: Users of LeRobot platform
identities.description: Systems running the Hugging Face LeRobot platform for AI and robotics are at risk of remote code execution due to CVE-2026-25874.

indicators.name: Anomalous LeRobot Child Process
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous LeRobot Child Process Spawn
id: 2b1c6d9e-8a7b-4f5c-9d0e-1a2b3c4d5e6f
status: experimental
description: Detects the LeRobot server process spawning suspicious child processes such as shells or network utilities, which may indicate remote code execution due to the exploitation of CVE-2026-25874.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/critical-unpatched-flaw-leaves.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-28
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.execution
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
level: critical
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts or custom integrations that intentionally spawn shells from the LeRobot process.
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '/lerobot-policy-server'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/sh'
      - '/bash'
      - '/powershell'
      - '/pwsh'
      - '/cmd.exe'
      - '/powershell.exe'
      - '/curl'
      - '/wget'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child
indicators.description: Detects the LeRobot server process spawning suspicious child processes like shells or network utilities, which is a strong indicator of RCE via CVE-2026-25874.
